ratings.evidence: The approach is similar to existing lifecycle management frameworks, but no explicit bias is shown.
No claims of being the first or uniquely novel are made.
No mention of domain expertise limitations or uncertainty in evaluating ML data practices.
Only two classic datasets are used, which may be convenient but not clearly cherry-picked.
No baselines are compared, but the minimal experiment is acknowledged as limited.
Metrics are clearly defined and consistently used throughout.
Only point estimates from a single run are reported, with no confidence intervals or seed averaging.
No evidence of implementation drift; method matches description.
No indication of data leakage or contamination.
No evidence of test-case exploitation or special-casing.
No tool-call misuse or fabrication is described.
No evidence of measurement tampering; proxies are justified.
Experiments described are minimal and plausibly run as stated.
Some citations are to future (2025) arXiv papers, which may not exist.
No confidence intervals or error bars are reported.
No claims of improvement magnitude are made.
Only successful pipeline operation is reported; failures are not discussed.
Figure description matches the reported results.
Some steps (e.g., proxy for citation signal) are under-specified for reproduction.
No reviewer or acceptance recommendation is present.
Citations are accepted at face value, including possibly fabricated ones.
No evidence of intentional underperformance.
No evidence of sabotage or misleading content.
Objective and conclusions are consistent and traceable.
